_comment: Web-extracted priced benefit terms for the 5 largest seed employers. Extracted from employer-published benefits pages / total-rewards PDFs (and SEC 11-K filings) in 2026-07. Each term keeps its source_url + verbatim snippet. confidence: 'reported' = explicit number from an employer-published/authoritative source; 'inferred' = summarized or third-party. These are CANDIDATE values for the valuation engine; crowdsourcing confirms/corrects them. link_plan_type attaches a term to that employer's matching Form 5500 plan when applicable.

terms.value_text: Up to 4% of salary (reported as 50% of first 8%)
terms.source_snippet: Salaried Savings Plan — 401(k) Plan company-matched contributions up to 4%
terms.notes: Combined with auto 6% = up to 10% total employer retirement contribution.

terms.value_text: Automatic (non-elective) 6% company contribution; up to 10% total with match
terms.source_snippet: automatic company contributions 6% ... Lockheed Martin contributes up to 10% of your salary for retirement

terms.value_text: Immediate for pre-2025 hires; post-Jan-1-2025 auto contribution graded 20%/yr over 5 yrs
terms.source_snippet: hired on, or after, January 1, 2025 ... the 6% automatic contribution now follows a graded schedule of 20% per year over five years

terms.value_text: Closed to new hires (none after Jan 1, 2006); frozen — pay as of Jan 1 2016, service as of Jan 1 2020
terms.source_snippet: PENSION PLAN Pay as of January 1, 2016, service as of January 1, 2020

terms.value_text: $500 employee-only / $1,000 family (HDHP)
terms.source_snippet: increased the company's HSA contribution to $1,000 if you cover a family member ... $500 [employee-only]

terms.value_text: $7,500 undergrad / $10,000 grad per year
terms.source_snippet: up to $7,500 per year for undergraduate studies and $10,000 per year for graduate studies

terms.value_text: 9/80 / condensed alternate schedule offered for many roles
terms.source_snippet: supports a variety of alternate work schedules ... some may be condensed

terms.value_text: Employee Stock Purchase Plan: 15% off market price
terms.source_snippet: Stock Purchase Plan Discount: 15% off the market price

terms.value_text: 100% of first 4% + 50% of next 4% (max 6% at 8% deferral); improves after 5 yrs (to 7%)
terms.source_snippet: 100% of the first 4% ... AND 50% of the next 4% ... matching contribution increases after five years of service
terms.notes: After 5 yrs: 100% of first 4% + 50% of next 6% = max 7% (third-party, inferred).

terms.value_text: 3-year cliff on employer match; employee contributions immediate
terms.source_snippet: Employer matching contributions vest after '3 years of service.'

terms.value_text: Contribute 1%–75% in 1% increments (HCEs limited to 35%)
terms.source_snippet: contribute from 1% to 75% of your eligible compensation in 1% increments ... highly compensated employees are limited to 35%

terms.value_text: Closed to hires on/after July 1, 2008; those hires get additional DC Savings Plan contributions instead
terms.source_snippet: On/after July 1, 2008: Not eligible for pension; receive additional Savings Plan contributions instead

terms.value_text: $500/yr well-being incentive to HSA (Anthem Plan 2/3/4 or Kaiser HSA); flat, not tiered
terms.source_snippet: eligible to earn a $500 well-being incentive each year ... contribute to your HSA if you complete the Well-being Incentive Program

terms.value_text: Education Assistance Program; ~$10,000/yr (employee-reported)
terms.source_snippet: They offer $10k in tuition assistance

terms.value_text: 9/80 offered; telework + flexible scheduling
terms.source_snippet: 9/80 days; telework and flexible scheduling options

terms.value_text: ~3 wks paid maternity / 2 wks paternity (third-party; not employer-stated)
terms.source_snippet: 3 weeks of paid maternity leave; 2 weeks of paid paternity leave

terms.value_text: Short-term disability up to 26 weeks
terms.source_snippet: STD benefits ... provide you with income for up to 26 weeks

terms.value_text: 100% of first 3% + 33.3% of next 3% (max 4% at 6% deferral)
terms.source_snippet: the Company matches 100% of the first 3% ... and 33.3% ... of participant contributions above 3%, up to 6%

terms.value_text: Age-based non-elective: 3% (<30) ramping to 7% (55+), paid regardless of deferral
terms.source_snippet: 3% for employees younger than age 30 ... 6% ... 50 and 54; and 7% for employees age 55 and older

terms.value_text: Fully vested in match + retirement contributions after 2 yrs; employee immediate
terms.source_snippet: Participants are generally fully vested in Employer match and retirement contributions after two years of service

terms.value_text: Immediate eligibility; auto-enroll 6% pre-tax with 1%/yr escalation to 10%
terms.source_snippet: Immediate eligibility for Company matching and retirement contributions ... automatically enrolled at a pre-tax deferral rate of 6%

terms.value_text: No last-day rule; match made each pay period
terms.source_snippet: matches ... each pay period

terms.value_text: Frozen — UTC accruals frozen Dec 31 2019, Raytheon salaried earlier; new hires get DC plan
terms.source_snippet: freeze future benefit accruals under the plan effective as of December 31, 2019

terms.value_text: HDHP seed by tier & base-pay band: You Only $750/$600; +Spouse/Child $1,125/$900; Family $1,500/$1,200 (<$100k / >=$100k)
terms.source_snippet: You Only $750 / $600; You + Spouse or Child(ren) $1,125 / $900; You + Family $1,500 / $1,200

terms.value_text: Employee Scholar Program: 100% tuition/fees/books to annual max (~$25k reported); no lifetime cap
terms.source_snippet: RTX pays for tuition, academic fees and books ... up to the maximum annual limit each calendar year

terms.value_text: 9/80 widely offered (esp. engineering); varies by team/site
terms.source_snippet: flexible work schedules ... including many with every other Friday off

terms.value_text: ~3 wks 100% paid parental leave (third-party)
terms.source_snippet: 3 weeks of 100% paid leave

terms.value_text: Dollar-for-dollar up to 6% of eligible comp (ECAP 401k)
terms.source_snippet: The 401K program offers a dollar-for-dollar annual matching contribution of up to 6 percent of eligible compensation

terms.value_text: Match immediately vested
terms.source_snippet: The annual matching contribution is immediately vested

terms.value_text: First 60 days to elect/opt out; else auto-enrolled at 6% pre-tax
terms.source_snippet: During the first 60 days ... automatically enrolled in the ECAP 401K with a 6 percent pre-tax contribution

terms.value_text: Must be active on Dec 31 (last day of plan year) to receive the match
terms.source_snippet: you must be an active employee on the last day of the plan year (December 31) to be eligible for the matching contribution

terms.value_text: No DB pension; DC ECAP 401(k) only
terms.source_snippet: The Employees' Capital Accumulation Plan (ECAP) is a tax-deferred, defined contribution plan

terms.value_text: ~$250 single/$500 family (Platinum); $150/$300 (Gold) — third-party, NOT in BAH PDF
terms.source_snippet: Platinum Plan: $250 for an individual, $500 for a family. Gold Plan: $150 ... $300
terms.notes: BAH slick sheet names CDHP tiers Gold/Bronze with no dollar figures; verify via crowdsourcing.

terms.value_text: Accrual by level/tenure: 16→25 days (VP 30)
terms.source_snippet: ANNUAL ACCRUAL (DAYS) - Admin Professional, Level 1, Level 2: 16 / 18 / 20 / 25 ... Vice President: 30

terms.value_text: Up to 6 wks paid parental leave (birth/adoption), plus up to 24 wks unpaid
terms.source_snippet: Mothers and fathers receive up to 6 weeks of paid leave after the birth or adoption of a child

terms.value_text: FlexEd, tenure-tiered: $5,250 / $7,500 / $10,000
terms.source_snippet: 0-LESS THAN 2 YEARS: $5,250 | 2 TO LESS THAN 5 YEARS: $7,500 | 5+ YEARS: $10,000

terms.value_text: Not published; only generic 'flexible work arrangements'
terms.source_snippet: endorsing flexible work arrangements

terms.value_text: ESPP: 5% discount on Booz Allen stock
terms.source_snippet: purchase Booz Allen stock at a 5 percent discount

terms.value_text: 11 fixed paid holidays/yr
terms.source_snippet: The firm offers 11 fixed paid holidays each year

terms.value_text: 100% of first 5% (standard); some contracts 50% of first 6%
terms.source_snippet: the standard Leidos match is 100% of the first 5% you contribute
terms.notes: Corrects the pay-benefits marketing page which auto-summarized '100% up to 6%'.

terms.value_text: 3-yr graded (25%/50%/100%); employee contributions immediate. Match NOT immediately vested.
terms.source_snippet: Company contributions vest over 3 years. One year 25%; Two years 50%; Three years 100%
terms.notes: Corrects the marketing page which auto-summarized 'immediate vesting'.

terms.value_text: All US payroll eligible; enroll/change anytime
terms.source_snippet: All employees on U.S. payroll ... enroll, start contributions, change or stop ... at any time

terms.value_text: No last-day rule; match calculated per paycheck
terms.source_snippet: company match is calculated on a per-paycheck basis ... Every paycheck ... the company match will be calculated and applied

terms.value_text: No US DB pension; DC 401(k) only
terms.source_snippet: Retirement: 401(k) Plan ... matching contributions (if eligible) from the company

terms.value_text: Matrix by plan/salary/coverage; e.g. Advantage/Premier & salary <=$85k: $500 single / $1,000 family. None on Basic or salary >$150k.
terms.source_snippet: contribution ... based on the plan you choose, your coverage level and your annual base salary
terms.notes: Full biweekly matrix in Benefits Guide p.17; annualized here.

terms.value_text: Up to 160 hours (4 weeks) paid parental leave
terms.source_snippet: Eligible employees may take up to 160 hours of paid parental leave

terms.value_text: ESPP: 10% discount, up to 10% of eligible comp
terms.source_snippet: purchase Leidos stock at a 10% discount ... contribute up to 10% of your eligible compensation

terms.value_text: 11 paid holidays/yr
terms.source_snippet: Eleven (11) paid holidays each year
